name: Forward Industries, Inc.
summary: Forward Industries, Inc. (FWDI) is a design company providing hardware and software product design and engineering services to medical and technology customers predominantly in the U.S., through subsidiaries including IPS and Kablooe. Services span electrical, mechanical, software, optical engineering, UX/UI design, and IoT architecture. The company also adopted Solana (SOL) as its primary treasury reserve asset in September 2025, pursuing staking, DeFi participation, and capital markets strategies within the Solana ecosystem.

products.name: Hardware and Software Product Design Services
products.description: End-to-end design and engineering from concept through prototype and manufacturing-ready files for consumer and industrial electronics.

products.name: Electrical Engineering Services
products.description: In-house electrical engineering for medical, industrial, and consumer electronics products.

products.name: Mechanical Engineering Services
products.description: In-house mechanical engineering supporting product design and development.

products.name: Software Engineering Services
products.description: In-house software engineering including mobile and enterprise applications.

products.name: UX/UI Design and Development
products.description: User experience and user interface design for electronics and software products.

products.name: IoT System Architecture
products.description: Design and architecture of Internet of Things systems for clients.

products.name: Optical Engineering Services
products.description: In-house optical engineering supporting cameras, lighting, and detection systems.

products.name: SOL Digital Asset Treasury
products.description: Acquisition, staking, and DeFi deployment of Solana (SOL) tokens as the company's primary treasury reserve asset.

partnerships.description: Asset management and services agreements to guide implementation of the digital assets treasury business.
White-label arrangements for operating SOL validators to earn staking rewards.
Multiple regulated third-party custodians hold SOL holdings with SOC 2-audited security controls.
